forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the Tasman Sea is responsible for northerly winds across South Australia ahead of a weakening cold front mid-week. A second cold front expected late in the week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers and isolated thunderstorm developing about the west during the day. Isolated showers extending to southern agricultural areas during the evening. Isolated showers about the northeast clearing at night. Maximum temperatures above average. Freshening north to northwesterly winds, shifting west to southwesterly in the far southwest in the evening.

Showers about central and eastern parts of the agricultural area, contracting eastwards and clearing later. Maximum temperatures above average, and nearer average in the west. North to northwesterly winds, ahead of a cooler west to southwesterly change about western and central parts, extending throughout apart from the far northeast during the day.
Dry, apart from isolated showers west of about Ceduna. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds, freshening in the west.

text.p: Showers in the far west extending over agricultural areas and western districts during the evening. Maximum temperatures above average, tending well above average in the north. North to northwesterly winds, freshening in the west and south.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: Generally, less than 5 mm about the agricultural area and west, increasing to 5 to 10 mm about southern and western coastal districts, isolated falls of 10 to 20 mm possible in these areas.
